title: Deterministic generation of cat states with more than $100$ photons under dissipation
abstract: Large-size cat states are especially meaningful and fundamental for exploring the quantum-to-classical transition, as well as promising resources for quantum metrology and fault-tolerant quantum computation. However, amplifying the magnitude of cat states remains challenging because of the growing fragility under decoherence. We propose to generate large cat states by using the dynamical invariant of hybrid qubit-bosonic systems under Hermitian or non-Hermitian time-dependent Hamiltonian. It is a study with the universal quantum control (UQC) theory, in which the system dynamics is analyzed in the ancillary picture via a unitary transformation conditional on the qubit state. The controllable dynamics that can be encoded in the evolution of the dynamical invariant is presented by the Heisenberg equation, which imposes constrains on the Hamiltonian. When the qubit is prepared in a balanced superposed state, the bosonic mode can evolve deterministically from the vacuum state to the cat state of a mean photon number over $120$. In the Hermitian case, the generation is perfect; and in the non-Hermitian case, the fidelity is over $0.962$. Our protocol can also be applied to the generation of the intrinsic cat states and the four-component cat states of large size. Through the preparation of macroscopic quantum states, our work essentially advances UQC to hybrid discrete-continuous variable systems.
summary: This paper presents a theoretical protocol for deterministically generating large-amplitude Schrödinger cat states in hybrid qubit-bosonic systems, even when dissipation is present. By leveraging the dynamical invariant within the framework of Universal Quantum Control, the authors achieve high-fidelity generation of states with over 100 photons. This advances the control theory for hybrid quantum systems, making large-scale quantum state preparation more feasible.
main_problem: The paper addresses the challenge of deterministically generating large-size Schrödinger cat states (with over 100 photons) while operating under the influence of dissipation.
main_result: A protocol was developed that deterministically generates these large cat states with high fidelity, achieving a mean photon number exceeding 120 in both Hermitian and non-Hermitian regimes.
method: The methodology utilizes Universal Quantum Control (UQC) theory by analyzing the dynamical invariant of the hybrid qubit-bosonic system under time-dependent, potentially non-Hermitian Hamiltonians.
model_or_system: The system modeled is a hybrid qubit-bosonic system, whose dynamics are governed by a time-dependent Hamiltonian, analyzed through the dynamical invariant $\mu(t)$ in the ancillary picture.
key_observables: Fidelity ($F$), mean photon number ($ ar{n}$), and the evolution of the dynamical invariant $\mu(t)$.
important_parameters: Mean photon number $ ar{n} > 100$; fidelity $>0.962$ (non-Hermitian); cat state amplitude $|\alpha|$; time evolution parameters $ eta(t)$ and $	heta(t)$.
main_assumptions: The analysis relies on transforming the system dynamics into a rotating frame and constraining the Hamiltonian such that the dynamical invariant can be controlled.
figures_summary: Figures illustrate the Wigner functions showing the interference pattern of the generated cat states, and plots track the fidelity, mean photon number, and trace of the density matrix across different stages of the generation protocol.
paper_structure: The paper builds from the general theory of UQC for hybrid systems, details the Hamiltonian and transformation into the rotating frame, derives the evolution of the dynamical invariant, and finally applies this framework to generate specific types of cat states (intrinsic and four-legged) in both closed and open systems.
why_interesting: This work provides a powerful theoretical extension of UQC theory to hybrid discrete-continuous variable systems, offering a robust method to create macroscopic quantum states essential for quantum metrology and computation despite environmental decoherence.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. Sketch of the open hybrid qubit-bosonic system, where the qubit is longitudinally coupled to the bosonic mode with the coupling strength g(t) and the phase ϕ. γe, γg and γm denote the gain or loss rates of the levels |e⟩and |g⟩and the bosonic mode, respectively. When γa = 0, a = e, g, m, it reduces to a closed qubit-bosonic system.

FIG. 2. Wigner functions of speciﬁc moments t/τ describing the evolution process of the resonator from vacuum state to cat state. The ﬁrst three panels of each line, e.g., (a-c), (e-g), and (i-k) are associated with the resonator states |ψb(t)⟩with various amplitudes or |α|, obtained by performing projective measurement on the basis |+⟩of the qubit. The relevant dynamics about the target state ﬁdelity and the mean photon number for the ﬁnal entangled states |Ψ(τ)⟩= (|C+ α (τ)⟩⊗|+⟩−|C− α (τ)⟩⊗|−⟩)/ √
FIG. 3. Dynamics of the density-matrix trace about the whole system Tr[ρ(t)], the ﬁdelity F(t) with respect to the target state |Ψ(τ)⟩= (|C+ α (τ)⟩⊗|+⟩−|C− α (τ)⟩⊗|−⟩)/ √

FIG. 4. (a) Dynamics of the density-matrix trace of Tr(ρ), the target state ﬁdelity F(t), and the mean photon num- ber ¯n of the ﬁnal entangled state |Ψ(τ)⟩in Eq. (42), and (b) Wigner function for the relevant four-legged cat state |ψb(τ)⟩= |C+ α (τ)⟩+ |C+ iα(τ)⟩at t = τ, obtained by a projec- tive measurement on |+⟩1|+⟩2 of the composite system. The parameters are set the same as Fig. 3(b) except θ(t) = 3πt/τ.
